channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: U.S. allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Sweden's worst mass shooting leaves at least 11 dead, including the gunman
item.description: OREBRO, Sweden (AP) — Sweden's worst mass shooting left at least 11 people dead, including the gunman, and at least five seriously wounded at an adult education center west of Stockholm.
item.dc:creator: ALEKSANDAR FURTULA, SERGEI GRITS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran's foreign minister responds to Trump 'maximum pressure' campaign amid regime panic
item.description: As President Donald Trump ramps up pressure on Iran, Tehran's foreign ministry announced a willingness to negotiate only the nuclear issue and not its support of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Witnesses describe chaos and bloodshed in Sweden’s worst mass shooting
item.description: Witnesses who survived Sweden’s worst mass shooting have described horror and panic that gripped an adult education center west of Stockholm as the gunman killed at least 10 people.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rwandan-backed rebels have seized another city in eastern Congo
item.description: Rwandan-backed rebels are gaining ground in eastern Congo despite the unilateral ceasefire they declared earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kabumba, Jean-Yves Kamale And Monika Pronczuk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italy says International Criminal Court made an 'immense mess' out of Libyan warlord arrest warrant
item.description: Italy’s justice minister has strongly defended the government’s decision to free and repatriate a Libyan warlord wanted by the International Criminal Court.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tlaib decries ‘fanatical bulls‑‑‑‘ from Trump on Gaza takeover
item.description: Progressive Rep. Rashida Tlaib (D-Mich.) spurned President Trump's recent suggestion that the U.S. take over the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, calling the idea “fanatical bulls‑‑‑.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump's Gaza plan has stunned the region. Here's a look at the serious obstacles it faces
item.description: President Donald Trump's stunning proposal to forcibly transfer hundreds of thousands of Palestinians out of the Gaza Strip and develop it as a tourist destination faces major obstacles.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hermoso's teammate and brother say in court the Spain player was pressured to downplay Rubiales kiss
item.description: The brother of Jenni Hermoso and one of her teammates have told a judge there were attempts to pressure the player into downplaying the unsolicited kiss by former Spanish federation president Luis Rubiales in the awards presentation ceremony following the 2023 Women’s World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Tales Azzoni, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's suggestion the US 'take over' the Gaza Strip is rejected by allies and adversaries alike
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's proposal that the United States “take over” the Gaza Strip and permanently resettle its Palestinian residents was swiftly rejected and denounced on Wednesday by American allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN chief sounds the alarm amid fears over possible DOGE-inspired cuts after Trump's order
item.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres sends a letter to UN staff as President Donald Trump makes sweeping changes to US foreign aid and funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philippine vice president impeached by House, faces Senate trial as political battle rages
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — The lower house in the Philippines impeached Vice President Sara Duterte Wednesday, accusing her of a wide range of crimes that included plotting to assassinate the president, large-scale corruption and failing to strongly denounce China's aggressive actions against Filipi
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico says it will not allow US to send Mexican migrants to Guantanamo Bay
item.description: Mexico told the the U.S. government it will not allow Mexican migrants to be sent to the Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, detention camp, the country's foreign minister said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thailand cuts power supplies to Myanmar border towns in effort to curb scam rings
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand on Wednesday cut off power supplies to areas in Myanmar where scam networks operate, following a public outcry demanding government action to tackle the problem.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Aga Khan, spiritual leader of Ismaili Muslims and a philanthropist, dies at 88
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Aga Khan, who became the spiritual leader of the world’s millions of Ismaili Muslims at age 20 as a Harvard undergraduate and poured a material empire built on billions of dollars in tithes into building homes, hospitals and schools in developing countries, died Tuesday. He was 88.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Saudi Arabia contradicts Trump, vows no ties with Israel without creation of Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia said it would not establish ties with Israel unless a Palestinian state is created, contradicting a suggestion from U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombia's president orders national oil company to cancel US venture over environmental concerns
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro on Tuesday ordered the state-run oil company Ecopetrol to cancel a joint venture with a U.S.-owned company that was expected to produce around 90,000 barrels of oil per day, citing environmental concerns.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Trump, Netanyahu joint presser
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday doubled down on his proposal for Palestinians to leave the Gaza Strip and announced that the U.S. will take over the tiny territory in a joint press conference alongside Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump's idea to clear Gaza is a real estate grab: Rep. Auchincloss
item.description: President Trump and his son-in-law view the Gaza Strip as “developable property," a Democratic lawmaker says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US has frozen some funding for the UN-backed mission to quell gangs in Haiti, UN says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.S. has notified the United Nations that it is freezing some funding to a U.N.-backed mission in Haiti tasked with fighting gangs trying to seize full control of the country’s capital, the U.N. said Tuesday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jewish population in West Bank keeps rising. Settlers hope Trump will accelerate growth
item.description: BEIT EL, West Bank (AP) — The Jewish population in the West Bank grew at twice the rate of the general Israeli population last year, according to an advocacy group that hopes the Trump administration will support policies that help accelerate the growth of settlements in the occupied territory.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greek island Santorini evacuated after earthquakes shake tourist destination
item.description: Hundreds of earthquakes that have rattled the Greek islands since last week have prompted the evacuation of thousands from the tourist hotspot of Santorini.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Denmark PM repeats Greenland ‘not for sale,’ but would welcome more American troops on Arctic island
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen said she would welcome President Donald Trump sending more U.S. troops to Greenland, which is "not for sale.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PM declares 'worst mass shooting in Swedish history' after gunman kills 10 on school campus
item.description: Swedish police are responding to a school shooting in Orebro, Sweden, on Tuesday after reports said at least 10 people were killed
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Trump orders creation of US sovereign wealth fund, says it could own part of TikTok
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday signed an executive order directing the U.S. to take steps to start developing a government-owned investment fund that he said could be used to profit off of TikTok if he's successful at finding it an American buyer.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Emergency crews deployed on Santorini as earthquake swarm worries Greek experts
item.description: More than 200 undersea earthquakes have been detected near the Greek island of Santorini over the past three days, worrying Greek experts and island residents alike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israelis and Palestinians rejoice after more hostages and prisoners are freed
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Eight more hostages were freed from the Gaza Strip by Hamas-led militants on Thursday in a sometimes chaotic process that briefly delayed Israel's release of 110 Palestinian prisoners and underscored the fragility of the ceasefire that began earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, WAFAA SHURAFA and ISABEL DeBRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Investigators recover black boxes from plane in DC crash
item.description: There were 60 passengers and four crew members aboard the plane, which was arriving from Wichita, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
